provenance.source_corpus: Evaluating the Barrier Assessment Technique Derived from FishXing Software and the Upstream Movement of Brook Trout through Road Culverts

source_spans.text: Comparison of movements of PIT-tagged Brook Trout in reference sites and culverts indicated that culverts impair fish passage.
Comparison of movements of PIT-tagged Brook Trout in reference sites and culverts indicated that culverts impair fish passage.
By comparing the range of passable flows between reference sites and stream culverts we determined there was a decreased range of passable flows through culverts.
The decreased range of passable flows in culverts A, B, and C support the presence of a velocity barrier.

FishXing predictions of suitable fish passage discharges were conservative, and tagged fish successfully navigated partial barriers that were at least 2–3 times the upper limits of stream flow predicted to allow successful passage.

nodes.name: stream flow predicted to allow successful passage

source_spans.text: FishXing predictions of suitable fish passage discharges were conservative, and tagged fish successfully navigated partial barriers that were at least 2–3 times the upper limits of stream flow predicted to allow successful passage.
The underestimation of fish swimming abilities can account for the conservative estimates by FishXing.
The underestimation of fish swimming abilities can account for the conservative estimates by FishXing.
